_comment: linear_proxy configuration (PyTorch full-plane engine), read by demo.py via load_config(). Standard JSON: documentation lives in ignored '_comment*' keys.

engine._comment: Bound engine settings. QoI variables are the data points of an entire x-y plane of the raw fields (n=65536 for a 256x256 plane; doubled for bilinear QoIs).
engine._comment_method: 'structured' = O(N) closed-form/low-rank bound (full plane); 'dense' = brute-force torch.func.hessian + box sampling (small planes only, for cross-validation).
engine._comment_device: 'cuda' (2x RTX A6000) or 'cpu'; auto-falls back to cpu if CUDA is unavailable.
engine._comment_dtype: Bounds are always computed in float64 for rigor; this key is informational.
engine._comment_sigma2_floor: wskew bound raises if the box could drive block variance sigma^2 below this (Hessian singularity).

validation._comment: Monte-Carlo: draw 'samples' uniform points in the box and check |F-g| <= t.

driver._comment: CLI driver defaults; each is overridable on the command line.
driver._comment_data: Directory of {FIELD}_*.raw files, or a .nc file; null -> synthetic field.
driver._comment_zlevel: Height index of the (y, x) plane to analyze.
driver._comment_abs_eb: Compression ABSOLUTE error bound on the field data points (uniform E).
driver._comment_run_self_tests: Run self-tests + cross-validation before the driver.

synthetic._comment: Synthetic field plane used when driver.data is null.
synthetic._comment_size: Generates a (size x size) plane -> N = size^2 variables.
